symptoms.symptom_name: Disabling fatigue with onset immediately after an uncomplicated viral URTI

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A clear temporal link between a viral illness and new disabling fatigue is the defining onset pattern of post-viral fatigue.
The tight chronological anchoring to an infection points to a post-infectious rather than a primary mood aetiology.

symptoms.symptom_name: Post-exertional malaise — delayed multi-day crash after minor physical or cognitive effort

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Post-exertional malaise is the hallmark feature distinguishing post-viral fatigue/ME-CFS from ordinary tiredness and from depression.
Discrete effort-triggered crashes with preserved desire to be active are not typical of depressive anergia.
Non-restorative sleep is a recognised core feature of post-viral fatigue syndromes.
Cognitive slowing and concentration difficulty are common associated features of post-viral fatigue.
Cognitive slowing is also compatible with hypothyroidism, which is why TFTs are part of the exclusion panel.

symptoms.symptom_name: Generalised myalgia and arthralgia without objective joint inflammation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Non-specific aches are common post-virally; the absence of true synovitis argues against inflammatory arthritis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Intermittent subjective feverishness with normal measured temperature

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Subjective feverishness without documented pyrexia is non-discriminating; a genuinely measured fever would prompt a search for occult infection instead.

symptoms.symptom_name: Caffeine-induced palpitations with no resting cardiac symptoms

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Palpitations after a recent viral illness mandate active cardiac screening even at low probability; here they are clearly caffeine-provoked with no resting symptoms, which is reassuring but does not exclude myocarditis without an ECG.

symptoms.symptom_name: No unintentional weight loss, no drenching night sweats, no focal neurological deficit (pertinent negatives)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of constitutional B-symptoms and focal neurology makes occult malignancy, chronic infection and a primary neurological cause much less likely, supporting the benign post-viral attribution.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal clinician-measured vital signs (BP 110/70, HR 82, RR 14, SpO2 99%, afebrile)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Stable haemodynamics with normal oxygenation make acute cardiac decompensation unlikely — but this is a sealed clinician measurement; the AI Doctor cannot obtain it and infers stability only from patient-reported observations.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal cardiopulmonary examination (no murmurs/rubs/gallops, lungs clear, no volume overload)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A normal cardiac and respiratory examination reduces the likelihood of myocarditis and heart failure — clinician-only finding, sealed from the AI Doctor.

symptoms.symptom_name: No active synovitis on joint examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of synovitis argues against inflammatory arthritis as the cause of the arthralgia — clinician-only examination finding.

symptoms.symptom_name: Screening blood panel (FBC, U&E, LFTs, TFTs, iron studies, CRP) — expected normal to support a diagnosis of exclusion

investigation_details.expected_result: Normal full blood count, electrolytes, liver and thyroid function, iron studies and CRP — a normal panel supports post-viral fatigue by excluding treatable organic causes (no raw values available via telehealth).
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: A treatable organic cause of fatigue (anaemia, thyroid disease, occult infection) could be missed and mislabelled as post-viral fatigue.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: TFTs are required to exclude hypothyroidism before attributing fatigue to a post-viral syndrome.
FBC and iron studies are required to exclude anaemia as a treatable cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: 12-lead ECG — expected normal or non-specific, used to help exclude myocarditis/arrhythmia

investigation_details.expected_result: Likely normal or non-specific; a normal ECG helps but does not fully exclude myocarditis, which is why cardiac red-flag safety-netting is retained.
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Subclinical cardiac involvement after a viral illness could go undetected.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An ECG is a reasonable first-line, patient-accessible test to screen for cardiac involvement given the recent viral illness and reported caffeine-provoked palpitations.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Post-infectious onset + post-exertional malaise + unrefreshing sleep + cognitive fog together form the characteristic post-viral fatigue pattern; no single feature is diagnostic but the constellation, once organic causes are excluded, is strongly suggestive.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Fatigue screen blood panel (FBC, U&E, LFTs, TFTs, iron studies, CRP)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: First-line exclusion of common treatable organic causes of profound fatigue before attributing to a post-viral syndrome; appropriately arranged as outpatient/non-urgent given stable observations.
Reasonable screen for cardiac involvement after a viral illness with reported palpitations; can be arranged promptly as an outpatient rather than via ED in a haemodynamically stable patient.

Not first-line here — there are no cardiac symptoms at rest and observations are stable; routing a stable patient to urgent cardiac biomarkers/ED is over-investigation driven by the alarming framing. Reserve for new cardiac red flags.
